source: Ash report (June 2026): 'gemini is always browned out'.
title: Gemini provider always browned out (retired gemini-2.0-flash free tier)
what_failed: Every Gemini call returned 429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED with free_tier limit 0 for gemini-2.0-flash, tripping the router's 5-min brownout on every attempt → effectively always browned out. Confirmed by live probe 2026-06-20. Secondary: GeminiProvider never set tokens_in/out, so cost/TPD tracking was always 0.
where_failed: core/providers/gemini.py (hardcoded model + no token counts); core/llm_router.py:98 default; brownout at core/llm_router.py:159/322.
why_likely: Google retired the free tier for gemini-2.0-flash (or the project's free quota is 0); the model id was hardcoded with no env override.
resolution: Bumped default model gemini-2.0-flash → gemini-2.5-flash in provider + router; added GEMINI_MODEL env override (authoritative) so Ash can point at a served model without touching guarded config.py; populated tokens_in/out from resp.usage_metadata. Unit tests in test_gemini_model_config.py. NOTE: if the Google project's free quota is 0 (billing-gated), Ash must set GEMINI_MODEL to a free-served model or enable billing — see T-213.
analysis: Brownout was a symptom; the cause was a retired/zero-quota model id. Env-configurability prevents recurrence when Google rotates models.
